output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, legal deadlines, and how to write a demand letter that gets results.
content_outline: Why Is My Landlord Withholding My Security Deposit?
How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit?
What Can a Landlord Legally Deduct From Your Security Deposit?
What To Do When Your Landlord Won't Give Your Deposit Back
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Works
Escalating Your Claim: Small Claims Court and Next Steps
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days after move-out.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear; only actual damage and unpaid rent are legally permissible.
A formal demand letter is often the fastest way to recover a withheld deposit without going to court.
If a landlord misses the state deadline, they may forfeit the right to keep any portion of the deposit.
output.cta: Stop waiting for a landlord who won't respond. Create your state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack in minutes and take back what's yours.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit
